title: Converting existing docker image to LXC
body:
Hi,
  I am scratching my head why do I need to create metadata.yaml and re-create a filesystem tarball if there are some tarballs inside the docker-ized image. The examples how to convert a docker image at skopeo website anticipate an image in docker hub:

`$ skopeo copy oci:busybox_ocilayout:latest dir:existingemptydirectory`
This is not my case.

Anybody willing to help me to convert https://www.dropbox.com/sh/y6bwuve685evmmp/AAD8fZM9XRoKZSS_R6aWPabma?dl=0 Linux image (11GB, essentially containing `BIOMEX_1.0-5/biomex.tar` which is sucked in normally by docker). It is linked from https://bitbucket.org/ftaverna/biomex/src/master/ and https://carmelietlab.sites.vib.be/en/biomex . It contains the following structure:

```
drwxr-xr-x root/root         0 2021-02-10 09:51 12ff20646bf784133471a1f756d05ad54d5babb88139ea2613b886038ac764af/

```

```
$ cat ../manifest.json 
[{"Config":"864d0b05e7cc9df6b8625eaff7a5e7c3d62ae338cfca4c292f801cd7c3eff5c0.json","RepoTags":["biomex:latest"],"Layers":["eecf5b5b4db7f87be31175f48d30a51008b9a178d3a1b1effc3bed32fa2ff637/layer.tar","99a25313f5fa5242f9adabadfba781b7aa35265502936e82cb9c1d498dc3d594/layer.tar","2f7ff73ef11845c54eb7983393ee87f5aeda4aa8c076eb510abd9e7348a4f9d9/layer.tar","7b6b42f810bcc791891b8d6099faa8ec8097180aa10cd68c8d7bb0b1b00b41fe/layer.tar","12ff20646bf784133471a1f756d05ad54d5babb88139ea2613b886038ac764af/layer.tar","8cadd00d64a3520231d0447e68dd4f866541e653a18c5ab798ef08b7a650fe3f/layer.tar","e76870c5139e073d29f4f62225102bcee48a561ddb3e541011d88147dc8c2ba7/layer.tar"]}]
$
```

I unpacked the subset tarballs and it one of the show it is `Debian 9`:

```
$ cat etc/debian_version
9.12
$ cat etc/os-release 
PRETTY_NAME="Debian GNU/Linux 9 (stretch)"
NAME="Debian GNU/Linux"
VERSION_ID="9"
VERSION="9 (stretch)"
VERSION_CODENAME=stretch
ID=debian
HOME_URL="https://www.debian.org/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://www.debian.org/support"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugs.debian.org/"
$ distro --root-dir .
Name: Debian GNU/Linux 9 (stretch)
Version: 9 (stretch)
Codename: stretch
$
```

Thank you.

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out.

This is probably just the wrong tool for the job; Skopeo is mostly a thin wrapper around the c/image library, which deals with copying images, and converting between image formats within the OCI container ecosystem. It’s not a tool to build images from scratch, or to deeply inspect it file by file. (Many of those tasks are implemented in Podman or Buildah.)

Nothing in the codebase deals with LXC codebases, and from the above it’s unclear to me, as someone completely unfamiliar with LXC formats, what exactly you are looking help with.

Wildly guessing, there’s a small chance you might be looking for `podman image mount`.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Please reopen if there’s more to discuss.

Hi, thank you, indeed, I got trapped with other approaches and I tried to use `podman` but somehow did not work initially. Currently am using plain `docker load` to inspect the contents ( https://bitbucket.org/ftaverna/biomex/issues/4/cannot-see-any-files-under-load-experiment ).
